primary_optimization: agent-to-agent discovery, delegation, task exchange, and secure collaboration
public_or_local_evidence_summary: A2A docs and README define an open protocol for communication between opaque agentic apps, Agent Cards, JSON-RPC over HTTP(S), streaming/push, text/file/JSON exchange, security/auth/observability, and Linux Foundation governance.
notes: SAB should publish an A2A standing profile rather than compete with A2A. Verified 2026-07-05: A2A defines an official extensions mechanism (AgentExtension objects in Agent Cards, A2A-Extensions activation header) but NO official trust/reputation extension exists; trust extensions are community proposals only (Discussion #1631 reputation-aware discovery; Issue #1628 trust.signals[]). ERC-8004 positions itself as the trust layer for A2A.

primary_optimization: onchain agent identity, reputation, and validation registries without pre-existing trust
public_or_local_evidence_summary: ERC-8004 (Draft, Standards Track: ERC) specifies three onchain registries: Identity (ERC-721/URIStorage agent handles resolving to registration files, format {namespace}:{chainId}:{identityRegistry}), Reputation (giveFeedback/revokeFeedback; 'the feedback submitter MUST NOT be the agent owner or an approved operator'), and Validation (validationRequest/validationResponse for stake-secured re-execution, zkML, or TEE oracles). Explicitly references A2A and MCP endpoints and three pluggable trust models.
notes: Closest external analog to SAB standing semantics and the #1 external threat. EIP status is Draft; ecosystem posts claim audited mainnet deployments on 20+ networks (UNVERIFIED at spec level). SAB posture: emit/consume 8004 feedback and validation records, not compete.

primary_optimization: provenance-attested builds and gated merges as de-facto trust for agent work
public_or_local_evidence_summary: Branch protection enforces required status checks, required PR reviews, CODEOWNERS approval, signed commits, push restriction, and linear history. Artifact attestations are 'built on Sigstore' and 'by itself provides SLSA v1.0 Build Level 2', verified via gh attestation verify; GitHub warns attestations 'are not a guarantee that an artifact is secure'. Sigstore = cosign + Fulcio short-lived certs + Rekor transparency log; SLSA v1.0 defines Build L1-L3 provenance levels.
notes: Attests provenance and process, not claim truth; no adversarial challenge, expiry, or revocable reliance. Single most likely absorber of agent trust workflows given distribution.

notes: Mechanism is competition scoreboards in narrow verifiable domains (trading), not challengeable claim standing. 'AgentRank' as a named mechanism was NOT confirmed in fetched docs (UNVERIFIED); live-vs-planned extent unclear.

category: decentralized identity and trust registry infrastructure
primary_optimization: DIDs, verifiable credentials, and monetized trust registries
public_or_local_evidence_summary: Product docs specify did:cheqd DIDs, verifiable credentials (JWT, JSON-LD, AnonCreds, SD-JWT), trust registries using the Decentralized Trust Chain model, DID-Linked Resources, status lists, and credential payments; partner SaaS (Paradym, Truvera, Hovi) consume the stack.
notes: Fetched docs are NOT agent-specific despite agent-facing marketing; trust registries answer who accredited whom, not whether an agent's work claims survive challenge.

primary_optimization: stake-weighted consensus scoring of machine work
public_or_local_evidence_summary: Yuma Consensus 'computes validator and miner emissions from validators' rankings of miners' performances'; consensus benchmark = 'the maximum weight level supported by at least a fraction kappa of total stake' (default 0.5); above-benchmark weights are clipped and over-weighting validators take bond penalty beta, so validators near consensus build stronger EMA bonds.
notes: Live crypto-economic validation of machine work at scale; scores continuous performance in subnets, not discrete challengeable claims with expiry/revocation; heavyweight tokenomics coupling.

primary_optimization: HTTP 402 pay-per-request rails for agents and APIs
public_or_local_evidence_summary: 'An open standard for internet native payments': server responds 402 with PAYMENT-REQUIRED header, client resubmits with signed payment payload, facilitator server verifies and settles onchain; TypeScript/Python/Go/Java implementations; originated at Coinbase, now under the x402 Foundation with the Coinbase repo as development fork.
notes: Payments, not trust — but standing plus payment rails is the composite stack agent economies need; complement to SAB rather than rival.

category: agent tool registry and namespace trust

public_or_local_evidence_summary: Official modelcontextprotocol registry ('like an app store for MCP servers'; preview with API freeze v0.1 since Oct 2025): server.json metadata, publisher CLI, namespace ownership proof via GitHub OAuth/OIDC or DNS/HTTP domain verification.
notes: Trust = namespace ownership only; no behavior or claim verification; SAB standing could layer on top of registry entries.

primary_optimization: directory-native agent identities with adaptive access and lifecycle governance
public_or_local_evidence_summary: Learn docs (updated 2026-06): agent identities and blueprints (parent-child templates) as first-class Entra constructs; OAuth 2.0, MCP, and A2A support; third-party agents (AWS Bedrock, n8n) via Auth SDK sidecar or workload identity federation; conditional access, risk detection, lifecycle governance, audit logging; 'available for all Microsoft Entra customers'; Microsoft Agent 365 is a separately licensed product for M365-wide agent operation.
